errors.description: Platform-admin surface reference to a non-existent Identity. NEVER surfaces to tenant callers — tenant-audience errors for missing identities are always folded into the generic `AUTH_FAILED` response (enumeration safety).
Registration or email-change attempt collided with an existing (non-erased) identity.

CompliantAgePolicy refused the Identity creation because dob resolves the applicant below the effective compliance floor (COPPA 13 in US; GDPR 13–16 per EU country). Response includes the applicable floor + region so downstream flows can route to the parent-consent workflow.
identity.check_lockout middleware refused. Response includes locked_until timestamp + unlock URL (password-reset flow).
Email verification required but not completed. Response includes resend URL.
Identity is soft-deleted (in the GDPR hold-period). Any login attempt against an erased identity should be routed to the generic AUTH_FAILED response for enumeration safety.
strong_password rule failed. Response body includes an array of `failed_requirements` so the SPA can highlight which policy element the caller violated.
haveibeenpwned reported the password in ≥ breach_check_threshold public data breaches. Response includes an actionable hint but NEVER the actual breach count (that stays in audit).
not_in_password_history rule failed. Response includes the configured history_size so the caller can understand why.
Reset token unknown, expired, or already consumed. Same error for all three cases (no info leak about token existence).
Verification token unknown, expired, or the identity is already verified. Idempotent — re-verifying a verified identity returns success (not this error).
MfaSecretEncryptor backend unreachable. Retryable at the caller; ops alerted on the metric side.
Entitlement gate refused (mfa_totp / mfa_webauthn not entitled) OR feature flag `identity.mfa_enrolment_enabled` is off.
Enrolment step: the submitted TOTP code did not match. Enrolment attempt discarded; caller may retry with a fresh code.
valid_recovery_code rule failed. Same message for wrong code / already-consumed code / no MFA enrolled.
identity.rate_limit_login middleware refused. Response includes Retry-After header + the throttle bucket (`ip` / `email`) so the SPA can render a proportional message.
Hard-delete attempt refused because an inbound User / PlatformUser row still references the Identity. Downstream cascade should have run first; investigate.
Restore-erased-identity refused because the hold-period has been extended by compliance.
